changes: Added runtime ownership metadata with runtime_id, owner_module, and active_contexts.
Added async context tracking and safe_shutdown_if_unused() to prevent shared teardown while contexts remain active.
Changed async restart paths to use guarded restart behavior instead of unconditional shared teardown.
Replaced direct sync Playwright stop usage with safe_runtime_stop() in WhatsApp runtime shutdown.
Registered sync runtime owners for WhatsApp and DM Playwright clients so shared sync shutdown only occurs when unused.
Added atomic_write_json() and atomic_append_jsonl() backed by temp-file fsync plus os.replace().
Migrated listed JSON and JSONL writers onto the shared atomic utility without changing formats or payload shape.
Used an internal atomic text helper for the existing JSONL compaction path in core/storage.py.
Added EngineCancellationToken and a compatibility stop-event wrapper.
Bound engine-specific tokens to active threads so existing STOP_EVENT checks prefer scoped cancellation.
Updated listener and worker-launch sites to propagate scoped cancellation into spawned threads.
Kept global STOP_EVENT fallback active for compatibility.
Added DashboardMetricsLoader with a worker thread and Qt signal handoff.
Moved dashboard file-read snapshot generation off the Qt thread.
Kept existing metric computation and UI update logic, but split snapshot collection from UI application.

details: Constructed MainWindow and triggered dashboard refresh without import/runtime errors.
Executed src.dm_campaign.proxy_workers_runner.run_dynamic_campaign() with a missing alias and received the expected safe early-return result.
Validated campaign-related modules and token plumbing via import plus compile smoke.
Reset the autoresponder runtime controller successfully.
Built an autoresponder summary payload from a BotStats instance without errors.
Verified atomic_write_json() writes and reloads JSON correctly in a temporary directory.
Verified atomic_append_jsonl() preserves append order in a temporary directory.

limitations: A live browser campaign was not executed because that would trigger account sessions, Playwright browsers, and network side effects.
A live autoresponder run was not executed because that would touch external APIs, sessions, and persisted conversation state.
Validation is therefore smoke-level for runtime bootstrapping and safety infrastructure, not a full end-to-end operational run.
